control: Enable Data Access (DATA_READ) audit logs on IP-bearing services + immutable org-level log sink
rationale: Admin Activity logs are always-on, but Data Access logs — the only record of who READ a Cloud Storage object, Secret Manager secret, or model artifact — are OFF by default for every service except BigQuery. Without them, read-based exfiltration of source/model weights leaves no trace.
how_to_check: gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORG_ID --format=json | jq '.auditConfigs' (expect DATA_READ/DATA_WRITE enabled for storage.googleapis.com, secretmanager.googleapis.com, aiplatform.googleapis.com, artifactregistry.googleapis.com); confirm an aggregated org-level Log Router sink to a locked Cloud Storage bucket and/or Google Security Operations; confirm the _Default bucket retention is extended past 30 days.
pass_criterion: Data Access (DATA_READ at minimum) audit logs are enabled via IAM auditConfigs on every service holding IP, exported by an aggregated org sink to a retention-locked bucket / Chronicle, with retention >=400 days.
remediation: Set auditConfigs at the org level for the IP-bearing services; create an aggregated sink (folder/org) an attacker in one project cannot disable; enable a locked log bucket (bucket lock) for tamper resistance; size ingestion cost and scope DATA_READ to sensitive services.

control: Enforce a VPC Service Controls perimeter around the projects and services holding IP
rationale: VPC Service Controls is GCP's marquee anti-exfiltration control: it blocks data egress to projects/identities outside the perimeter even with valid stolen credentials. It is opt-in and must be ENFORCED (not dry-run) and must cover the exact IP-bearing services.
how_to_check: gcloud access-context-manager perimeters list; describe the perimeter (status, not spec, = enforced) and confirm restrictedServices includes storage.googleapis.com, bigquery.googleapis.com, aiplatform.googleapis.com, artifactregistry.googleapis.com, secretmanager.googleapis.com; review ingress/egress rules for over-broad allows.
pass_criterion: An enforced (not dry-run) service perimeter encloses all projects holding IP, restricts the IP-bearing services, and has tightly-scoped ingress/egress rules; access levels gate trusted networks/identities.
remediation: Stand up the perimeter in dry-run first to surface breakage, tune ingress/egress rules, then enforce; add access levels for corp networks; verify Vertex AI / Artifact Registry are in restrictedServices; pair with restricted.googleapis.com VIP + Private Google Access.

control: Eliminate service-account JSON keys; adopt Workload Identity Federation with tight attribute conditions
rationale: Long-lived SA JSON keys never expire and are GCP's top credential-theft vector. WIF removes them for CI/CD and external workloads — but an over-broad provider attribute condition (e.g. trusting an entire GitHub org, or pull_request-from-fork) is a confused-deputy hole.
how_to_check: Check org policy: gcloud org-policies describe iam.managed.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation --organization=ORG_ID (and disableServiceAccountKeyUpload) = enforced; inventory existing keys (SA key age); for each WIF provider, review attributeCondition for an exact repository/branch match (assertion.repository, assertion.ref) and that pull_request events from forks cannot mint tokens.
pass_criterion: SA key creation/upload disabled org-wide; no human or CI uses a downloaded JSON key; every WIF provider pins an exact subject/attribute condition (no wildcard org trust, fork PRs excluded).
remediation: Enforce the key-creation/upload constraints; migrate CI to WIF (GitHub Actions OIDC -> impersonate a least-privileged SA); tighten attributeCondition to assertion.repository==exact and guard ref/event; rotate out and delete existing keys.

control: Stamp out basic roles and the default-SA Editor grant — including on orgs created before May 3, 2024
rationale: Basic roles (Owner/Editor/Viewer) are project-wide and Google itself warns against them; the Compute Engine default SA historically auto-receives Editor. The secure-by-default baseline (automaticIamGrantsForDefaultServiceAccounts etc.) only auto-applies to orgs created on/after May 3, 2024 and is NOT retroactive — an established org inherits the legacy footgun.
how_to_check: gcloud organizations get-iam-policy / project IAM for roles/owner+roles/editor on user and default-SA principals; gcloud org-policies describe iam.automaticIamGrantsForDefaultServiceAccounts --organization=ORG_ID = enforced; check the *-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com binding for roles/editor.
pass_criterion: No human principals hold basic roles in production projects; the default SA holds no Editor (or is unused with dedicated per-workload SAs); the automaticIamGrants and SA-key constraints are enforced across the whole hierarchy, not just new orgs.
remediation: Enforce the secure-by-default constraints manually on the existing org/folders; replace default-SA Editor with least-privilege roles (enforcing the constraint does NOT revoke existing grants); move workloads to dedicated per-service SAs; use IAM Recommender to right-size.

control: Security Command Center Premium/Enterprise org-wide for threat detection and posture
rationale: SCC Standard is thin and eroding (the Feb 2026 'enhanced Standard' migration narrows it); the behavioral detectors that matter — Event Threat Detection, Container/VM Threat Detection, attack-path/exposure and posture-as-code — are tier-gated to Premium/Enterprise.
how_to_check: gcloud scc settings services describe / confirm the SCC tier at the org level; confirm Event Threat Detection, Container Threat Detection, and Security Health Analytics are active and that findings export to your SIEM; confirm auto-coverage of new projects.
pass_criterion: SCC Premium or Enterprise is enabled at the organization with Event/Container/VM Threat Detection and Security Health Analytics on, findings exported, and new projects auto-covered.
remediation: Activate SCC Premium/Enterprise at the org; enable the threat-detection services; wire findings to Chronicle/SIEM and ticketing; enable Security Posture service for posture-as-code drift detection.

control: Apply the full Org Policy guardrail baseline across the whole hierarchy
rationale: Org Policy is GCP's rich SCP-equivalent, but NOTHING is enforced by default on orgs predating the secure-by-default cutoff, and even the new baseline omits network/exposure constraints (e.g. skipDefaultNetworkCreation, external-IP, OS Login).
how_to_check: gcloud org-policies list --organization=ORG_ID; confirm enforcement of compute.skipDefaultNetworkCreation, compute.vmExternalIpAccess (deny), compute.requireOsLogin, iam.allowedPolicyMemberDomains, gcp.resourceLocations, and the SA-key constraints at org/folder level.
pass_criterion: A documented baseline of org policies (network suppression, deny external IP, require OS Login, domain-restricted sharing, allowed locations, SA-key restrictions) is enforced org-wide and inherited by all projects.
remediation: Author the baseline as policy-as-code; roll out at the org node so all folders/projects inherit; delete pre-existing default networks; use Policy Analyzer to verify effective policy.

control: Lock down serverless exposure: block allUsers, restrict ingress, require invoker IAM
rationale: Cloud Run/Functions are private by default, but 'Allow unauthenticated' is a one-toggle public endpoint (allUsers), default ingress 'all' lets the run.app URL bypass the load balancer / Cloud Armor, and the org-policy invoker guardrail is opt-in.
how_to_check: gcloud org-policies describe iam.allowedPolicyMemberDomains (domain-restricted sharing blocks allUsers) and constraints/run.managed.requireInvokerIam = enforced; per service: gcloud run services describe SVC --format='value(spec.template.metadata.annotations)' for ingress=internal-and-cloud-load-balancing and no allUsers in the invoker policy.
pass_criterion: Domain-restricted sharing and run.managed.requireInvokerIam are enforced org-wide; public services intentionally exposed sit behind an external LB with ingress restricted to internal-and-cloud-load-balancing; no unintended allUsers invoker bindings.
remediation: Enforce iam.allowedPolicyMemberDomains and run.managed.requireInvokerIam; set ingress to internal-and-cloud-load-balancing for services that should only be reached via the LB; audit invoker IAM for allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers.

control: Use CMEK in a dedicated key project with key-IAM separation for IP-bearing data
rationale: Default Google-managed encryption gives ZERO cross-project key-IAM isolation. CMEK in a separate key project, with key IAM held by a different team than the data, creates an independent blast-radius boundary; Cloud HSM/EKM add hardware/sovereignty custody.
how_to_check: Confirm IP-bearing buckets/disks/datasets use CMEK (not Google-managed): gcloud storage buckets describe / disk describe for the kmsKeyName; confirm the keys live in a dedicated key project whose Cloud KMS IAM is separated from the data project's admins; consider Cloud HSM/EKM for the most sensitive keys.
pass_criterion: Crown-jewel data (model weights, source, training datasets) is CMEK-encrypted with keys in a separate key project, key IAM held by a distinct principal set, rotation scheduled; the org policy requiring CMEK is enforced on supported services.
remediation: Provision a dedicated key project; create CMEK keys with separated IAM and rotation; enforce the constraints/gcp.restrictNonCmekServices / require-CMEK org policies; adopt Cloud HSM or EKM where key custody/sovereignty is required.

control: Turn on Binary Authorization enforce-mode + Artifact Analysis for GKE and Cloud Run
rationale: Binary Authorization is a genuine NATIVE deploy-time attestation gate (a GCP strength worth actually using) — but it is opt-in and ships in dry-run; unsigned/unattested images deploy until you enforce.
how_to_check: gcloud container binauthz policy export — confirm enforcementMode=ENFORCED_BLOCK_AND_AUDIT_LOG (not dry-run), a default-deny rule with required attestors, and minimal breakglass; confirm Artifact Analysis scanning is on and feeds the attestor; confirm Cloud Run deploy-time check is enabled.
pass_criterion: Binary Authorization is in enforce mode for GKE and Cloud Run with attestation required from a build/scan attestor, breakglass restricted and audited, and Artifact Analysis vulnerability scanning feeding the gate.
remediation: Define the policy with required attestors; run dry-run to surface unsigned workloads, then flip to enforce; wire Cloud Build SLSA provenance + Artifact Analysis into attestation; restrict and alert on breakglass.

control: Put Cloud Armor on every external load balancer and stop run.app direct-URL bypass
rationale: Cloud Armor WAF/Adaptive Protection only protects traffic that flows through an external load balancer with an attached security policy; a Cloud Run default ingress 'all' run.app URL bypasses it entirely — relevant to GPU/inference cost-abuse.
how_to_check: gcloud compute security-policies list and confirm each external backend service has a policy attached (WAF managed rules + rate-based rules + Adaptive Protection); confirm Cloud Run services behind the LB have ingress=internal-and-cloud-load-balancing so the run.app URL can't be hit directly.
pass_criterion: Every internet-facing LB has a Cloud Armor policy (managed WAF rules, rate-based throttling sized to per-request GPU cost, Adaptive Protection); no GPU-backed service is reachable via its raw run.app URL.
remediation: Attach Cloud Armor policies to all external backends; add rate-based rules and preconfigured WAF rules; enable Adaptive Protection; restrict Cloud Run ingress so traffic must traverse the LB.

control: Enable Access Transparency and Access Approval over IP-bearing workloads
rationale: Visibility into GOOGLE'S OWN staff access to your content is off until Access Transparency is enabled (now positioned as a default control but still needs an enablement action + a qualifying paid support tier); Access Approval adds consent. Coverage for Vertex AI / AI services has gaps worth confirming.
how_to_check: Confirm Access Transparency is enabled at the org and AxT logs appear in Cloud Logging; confirm Access Approval is configured with notification/approval routing; check the AxT/Access Approval supported-services list for Vertex AI and the specific services holding IP.
pass_criterion: Access Transparency logs are flowing and Access Approval is enforced for the services holding IP, with documented coverage gaps (e.g. any AI service not yet supported) accepted explicitly.
remediation: Enable Access Transparency (ensure a qualifying support tier); configure Access Approval with approver routing and break-glass policy; track which IP-bearing services are not yet covered and compensate.

control: Network egress + private access controls: Flow Logs, Cloud NAT, egress filtering, Private Google Access
rationale: VPC Flow Logs are off by default and egress to the internet is allowed by default — a direct exfil path for proprietary IP. Private Google Access + restricted VIP keep API traffic off the public internet.
how_to_check: Confirm VPC Flow Logs enabled on subnets carrying sensitive workloads; confirm egress firewall rules / Secure Web Proxy / VPC-SC egress restrict outbound; confirm Cloud NAT (no public IPs on workload VMs) and Private Google Access + restricted.googleapis.com routing.
pass_criterion: Sensitive subnets have Flow Logs on, no workload VMs hold external IPs (egress via Cloud NAT), outbound is filtered (egress firewall / Secure Web Proxy / VPC-SC), and Google API traffic uses Private Google Access + the restricted VIP.
remediation: Enable Flow Logs; remove external IPs and route egress through Cloud NAT; add egress firewall rules or Secure Web Proxy domain allowlisting; enable Private Google Access and restricted.googleapis.com; pair with the VPC-SC perimeter.
